title: fraxinus pennsylvanica subsp. novae-angliae
intro: Fraxinus pennsylvanica subsp. novae-angliae, also known as the New England green ash, is a subspecies of the white ash tree native to eastern North America. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of Fraxinus pennsylvanica subsp. novae-angliae include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being.
